title: SAS Viya Sizing Discovery Questions
description: Ask these questions to understand the customer's infrastructure requirements. The answers help determine whether they need a Small, Medium, or Large Viya workload profile. For exact specifications, refer to the official SAS sizing guides for their target cloud platform.
note: Only SAS Viya has official sizing guides. Industry solutions (Fraud, AML, Risk, etc.) run ON Viya and their sizing is determined by the Viya tier + workload size.

scoringGuide.logic: The HIGHEST tier selected across all questions determines the minimum recommendation. If most answers are Small but one critical dimension (e.g., transaction volume or availability) requires Large, recommend Large.
scoringGuide.outputTemplate: Based on your requirements, you likely need a **{size}** workload profile on **SAS Viya {tier}**. Contact SAS or VST ECS for the detailed sizing guide specific to your cloud platform.

questions.question: What is the total size of data that will be loaded onto the Viya environment?
questions.context: This is all data across all Viya offerings — assume uncompressed, UTF-8 encoded. Include data loaded onto disk (not just what's in memory).

answers.label: Up to 250 GB
answers.description: Single data source or small department dataset

answers.label: 250 GB - 1 TB
answers.description: Multiple data sources, larger datasets, multi-department

answers.label: 1 TB+
answers.description: Enterprise-wide data, many sources, multi-TB volumes

questions.question: How much data needs to be loaded INTO memory (CAS) at any given time?
questions.context: CAS loads data into memory for fast processing. This is the largest dataset you'll analyze interactively — not total disk storage.

answers.label: Up to 75 GB in memory
answers.description: Moderate analytical datasets, simple models

answers.label: 75 GB - 500 GB in memory
answers.description: Larger datasets, complex models, multiple concurrent loads

answers.label: 500 GB+ in memory
answers.description: Very large datasets, enterprise analytics, many concurrent CAS sessions

questions.question: How many users will be working on the platform concurrently during peak hours?
questions.context: Count active analytical sessions — designers exploring data, programmers running code, viewers on dashboards. Not total named users.

answers.label: Up to 10 concurrent sessions
answers.description: Small team, focused usage

answers.label: 10-25 concurrent sessions
answers.description: Departmental team, mix of designers and viewers

answers.label: 25+ concurrent sessions
answers.description: Enterprise-wide, many parallel users and batch jobs

questions.question: What is the size of your LARGEST single dataset that users will interact with?
questions.context: The single biggest table or dataset that will be loaded into CAS for analysis. This is the critical sizing driver for CAS memory nodes.

answers.label: Up to 5 GB
answers.description: Manageable datasets, departmental scope

answers.label: 5 - 50 GB
answers.description: Substantial tables requiring significant memory

answers.label: 50 GB+
answers.description: Very large tables — transaction histories, CDRs, full customer databases

questions.question: What types of analytical workloads will run on the platform?
questions.context: Batch = scheduled overnight jobs. Interactive = users exploring data in real-time. Real-time scoring = API-based sub-second decisioning. Each requires different infrastructure.

answers.label: Batch processing + light interactive
answers.description: Scheduled jobs with some dashboard viewing

answers.label: Batch + interactive + near-real-time scoring
answers.description: Mixed workloads with API scoring under 1 second

answers.label: All: batch + interactive + real-time + streaming
answers.description: Full spectrum including sub-100ms decisioning and event stream processing

questions.question: Which SAS Viya offerings will be deployed?
questions.context: More offerings running simultaneously require more infrastructure. Each offering (VA, VDMML, Model Manager, Intelligent Decisioning) adds resource requirements.

answers.label: 1-2 offerings (e.g., VA + Model Studio)
answers.description: Focused deployment with limited scope

answers.label: 3-5 offerings (e.g., VA + VDMML + Stats + Model Manager)
answers.description: Department using multiple capabilities

answers.label: Full platform (all offerings including ID and ESP)
answers.description: Enterprise deployment with all Viya capabilities active

questions.question: What are the availability requirements?
questions.context: Higher availability = more infrastructure (multi-AZ, failover nodes). Production-critical systems need higher resilience than development environments.

answers.label: Business hours availability acceptable
answers.description: Planned maintenance windows during off-hours are fine

answers.label: High availability with failover
answers.description: Multi-AZ deployment, auto-recovery from node failures

answers.label: Mission-critical — near-zero downtime
answers.description: Active-active CAS, full HA/DR, continuous operation required

questions.question: Which cloud platform (or on-premises) will you deploy on?
questions.context: SAS provides specific sizing guides for each platform with recommended instance types and configurations.

answers.label: AWS (Amazon EKS)
answers.description: Official sizing guide available for EKS

answers.label: Microsoft Azure (AKS)
answers.description: Official sizing guide available for AKS

answers.label: Google Cloud (GKE)
answers.description: Official sizing guide available for GKE

answers.label: Red Hat OpenShift
answers.description: Official sizing guide available for OpenShift

answers.label: On-premises / Other Kubernetes
answers.description: Contact SAS for custom sizing

nextSteps: Once you've determined the workload size (Small/Medium/Large) and Viya tier (Standard/Advanced/Enterprise), contact SAS or VST ECS Philippines for the detailed hardware sizing guide specific to your cloud platform. The official guides include exact instance types, node counts, storage configurations, and networking requirements.
contact: VST ECS Philippines — SAS Partner | Contact your SAS account team for official sizing documentation
